questions.text: Choose the correct passive voice:How did she defraud him of his savings?
questions.subject: English

options.text: How had he been defrauded of his savings?
options.explanation: This option is in the past perfect passive voice. While it maintains the passive construction, it introduces the past perfect tense, suggesting that the action of being defrauded occurred before a certain point in the past.

options.text: How had he been defrauded by her?
options.explanation: This option also uses the past perfect passive voice, but it specifies the agent of the action by using the preposition “by her”. However, the original sentence does not mention or emphasize the agent of the fraud.

options.text: How was he defrauded of his savings?
options.explanation: This option correctly transforms the sentence into the passive voice by using the verb “was defrauded” indicating the action being done to the subject “he”. The prepositional phrase “of his savings” remains intact as it represents what was taken from him.

options.text: How was he defrauded by her of his savings?
options.explanation: This option uses the passive voice and introduces the agent “by her”. This phrasing suggests that the savings were taken by the agent, which is not the intended meaning of the original sentence.

questions.explanation: Option D is the only sentence, that has all the components of the main sentence, hence we will go with that option.

questions.text: You are called names by him.[Choose the correct voice:]
questions.subject: English

options.text: He is calling you names.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: He calls you names.
options.explanation: This option is correct. When the passive voice is in past simple tense the active voice is written in present simple tense.

options.text: He called you names.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: You are being called names by him.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: When the passive voice is in past simple tense the active voice is written in present simple tense. Hence B is the correct answer.

questions.text: The principal has forbidden smoking on the campus.Select the correct passive voice:
questions.subject: English

options.text: Smoking has been forbidden on the campus by the principal.
options.explanation: This is correct. When active voice is converted into passive voice the place of subject and object are interchanged in the sentence and there is no change in the tense however 3rd form of the verb is used in the passive voice.

options.text: Smoking had been forbidden on the campus by the principal.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: Smoking was being forbidden on the campus by the principal.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: It is forbidden by the principal to smoke on campus.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: When active voice is converted into passive voice the place of subject and object are interchanged in the sentence and there is no change in the tense however 3rd form of the verb is used in the passive voice. Hence the best possible answer is A.

questions.text: Saba was sick on the bus.[The underlined prepositional phrase functions as a _ in this sentence:]
questions.subject: English

options.text: Adjunct
options.explanation: Adjuncts are regular adverbs of time, place, manner, degree, and frequency. They are essential to the meaning of the sentence, however, the phrase, “on the bus” has no clear impact on the sentence and the sentence won’t lose its meaning if we drop the phrase.

options.text: Disjunct
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Disjunct expresses the writer’s opinion like “sadly”, “realistically” etc.

options.text: Conjunct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Conjuncts link two clauses or sentences such as “however” etc.

options.text: Adverbial
options.explanation: Adverbials give more information about verbs and even if dropped, it won’t have a significant impact on the sentence hence D is the right answer.

questions.explanation: Adverbials give more information about verbs and even if dropped, it won’t have a significant impact on the sentence hence D is the right answer.

questions.text: She needs to clean the room. (Choose the correct passive voice):
questions.subject: English

options.text: The room needed to clean by her.
options.explanation: Through means moving from one end or side of something to the other. Since this preposition suits the given sentence best, so Option C is correct.

options.text: The room needed to be cleaned by her.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it uses the past tense ("needed") instead of the present tense ("needs") and does not match the original sentence. The correct form for the passive voice would be "needs to be cleaned."

options.text: The room needs to be clean by her.
options.explanation: This option uses the incorrect form of the verb "clean." In the passive voice, we use the past participle form of the verb ("cleaned") instead of the base form ("clean").

options.text: The room needs to be cleaned by her.
options.explanation: This option correctly uses the passive voice. It uses the auxiliary verb "be" in combination with the past participle form of the verb "cleaned" to form the passive construction.

questions.explanation: Option D correctly uses the passive voice. It uses the auxiliary verb "be" in combination with the past participle form of the verb "cleaned" to form the passive construction.
